purpose: Canonical scientific-claims matrix for public project wording.

status_legend.implemented: Implemented in the codebase, but not externally validated as valuable.
status_legend.controlled_evidence: Supported by controlled benchmark evidence under a narrow protocol.
status_legend.strong_intra_theory_evidence: Strongly supported inside the theory the implementation operationalizes.
status_legend.early_controlled_evidence: Initial comparative or task evidence exists, but remains narrow or early.
status_legend.not_established: Not supported by current public evidence.

claims.claim_text: AFT is implemented as a coherent multi-layer memory engine.
claims.allowed_public_wording: AFT is implemented as a coherent multi-layer memory engine.
claims.current_evidence: Public API, engine/retrieval/state modules, sync/async parity tests.
claims.next_study: Keep API/docs aligned while external evaluations grow.

claims.current_evidence: 126 fidelity cases validate affect-aware ranking logic. The controlled quadrant benchmark (SBERT) ties AFT and naive_cosine at recall@5 = 0.80 (ceiling effect, N = 20 items) while clearly beating recency (Δ = −0.55, p < 0.001). The realistic replay benchmark (SBERT) shows AFT top1 = 0.70 vs naive_cosine = 0.50 (N = 100). Appraisal confound study (2026-04-26): aft_noAppraisal (architecture only, no appraisal) = 0.78 vs naive_cosine = 0.55, Δ ≈ +0.23 — descriptive architecture-attribution evidence consistent with S2.
claims.not_yet_shown: General downstream superiority over production memory systems.
claims.next_study: Expand external and realistic retrieval evaluations.

claims.claim_text: The implementation is faithful to the theories it operationalizes.
claims.allowed_public_wording: The implementation is faithful to the theories it operationalizes.
claims.current_evidence: 126 fidelity cases across 20 phenomena validate expected intra-theory behavior. Ablation study (SBERT, N=100, pre-reg v3 Addendum E + Addendum F) now covers all 7 AFT mechanisms: dual-path encoding (He1 FAIL expected; Hf1 PASS — dual_path 0.35 > aft_keyword_synchronous 0.07; deferral mitigates synchronous keyword destruction), APE-gated reconsolidation (He2 FAIL; null result — benchmark does not exercise reconsolidation triggers). 8-variant ablation run 2026-04-27.
claims.not_yet_shown: Ecological correspondence to human emotional memory. LLM-appraisal dual-path advantage (requires non-destructive appraisal dataset; Addendum G future).
claims.next_study: Human and behavioral validation. Addendum G: dual-path ablation with LLMAppraisalEngine on dataset without preset affect injection.

claims.current_evidence: The appraisal-quality benchmark provides early controlled evidence on natural-language inputs.
claims.not_yet_shown: Calibration across models, domains, and languages.
claims.next_study: Appraisal robustness study across models, domains, and languages.

claims.claim_text: AFT helps on replayable multi-session memory tasks.
claims.allowed_public_wording: AFT helps on replayable multi-session memory tasks (v2, N=200, SBERT: AFT top1=0.53 vs naive_cosine=0.33, Δ=+0.21 [0.15,0.27], p<0.001; e5-small-v2: AFT top1=0.50 vs 0.34, Δ=+0.16 [0.09,0.22], p<0.001).
claims.current_evidence: Realistic replay v2 (50 scenarios, 200 queries, 5 challenge types × 40). SBERT bge-small-en: AFT top1=0.53 vs naive_cosine=0.33, Δ=+0.205 [0.150,0.265], p_bootstrap<0.001, d=0.49. e5-small-v2: AFT top1=0.50 vs naive_cosine=0.34, Δ=+0.155 [0.090,0.225], p_bootstrap<0.001, d=0.31. Advantage holds on both embedder classes. Architecture attribution confirmed: Hd1 PASS (Addendum D, seed=1, 2026-04-27); Gate 3 CLOSED.
claims.not_yet_shown: Performance on external open-domain QA (LoCoMo Gate 1 FAIL — AFT F1=0.168 vs naive_rag=0.271). Multilingual generalization.
claims.next_study: Add Italian multilingual slice (G6). Architecture attribution confirmed via Hd1 (Gate 3 CLOSED).

claims.claim_text: AFT does not outperform naive RAG on external open-domain conversational QA (LoCoMo).
claims.allowed_public_wording: On the LoCoMo conversational QA benchmark (1986 QA pairs, 10 conversations), AFT retrieval underperforms a naive RAG baseline (F1 0.168 vs 0.271; judge_acc 0.279 vs 0.441). Gate 1 was not met. AFT's affective weighting does not help on factual open-domain QA.
claims.current_evidence: Pre-registered S1 run completed 2026-04-27. H1 and H2 both fail (Δ<0). Holm-corrected one-tailed p=1.0 for both hypotheses.
claims.not_yet_shown: Whether a task-specific configuration of AFT (lower mood weight, affect-neutral retrieval) could match baseline on factual QA.
claims.next_study: Investigate per-task retrieval weight tuning; document as scope limitation in paper.

claims.claim_text: The system models human emotional memory.
claims.allowed_public_wording: The system is theory-inspired, but does not yet have human or ecological validation.
claims.next_study: Pilot human evaluation with completed ratings and external benchmarks.
